messages.content: You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
Sou cloud architect e preciso reduzir custos OCI para TechCorp Brasil no projeto ecommerce-migration, em production/sa-saopaulo-1, considerando sem downtime. Qual abordagem voce recomenda?

**Analise de Custos — Cost Optimization (TechCorp Brasil - ecommerce-migration)**

**Componentes de custo:**
1. **Recurso principal**: Based on usage/provisioned capacity
2. **Storage associado**: Volumes, backups, snapshots
3. **Networking**: Data transfer entre regioes e internet
4. **Servicos complementares**: Monitoring, Logging, Vault

**Otimizacoes recomendadas:**
- Use OCI Budgets para alertas de gasto
- Tag resources por projeto para chargeback
- Revise recursos nao utilizados semanalmente
- Considere Universal Credits para descontos por commitment

**Referencia:** [OCI Pricing Calculator](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/costestimator.html)

**Dicas de otimizacao:**
- Monitore utilizacao real antes de dimensionar
- Automatize start/stop de ambientes non-production
- Use OCI Cost Analysis reports mensalmente
- Avalie serverless (Functions, Autonomous) para workloads variaveis

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Billing/Concepts/costoverview.htm
You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
Como identificar desperdicios em recursos OCI no OCI para DataFlow Solutions no contexto de data-lake-modernization, com cenario brownfield e requisito de sem IP público?

Guia de boas praticas para cost-optimization - DataFlow Solutions - data-lake-modernization:

**Planejamento inicial:**
- Defina claramente os requisitos de cost-optimization
- Escolha a regiao `us-ashburn-1` baseada em latencia e compliance
- Use o compartment `development` para isolamento logico
- Documente arquitetura e dependencias

**Fase de implementacao:**
- Use Infrastructure as Code (Terraform recomendado)
- Implemente tagging consistente:
  - `project: DataFlow Solutions - data-lake-modernization`
  - `environment: development`
  - `managed-by: terraform`
- Configure monitoring desde o inicio
- Implemente backup automatizado

**Seguranca aplicada:**
- Aplique principio de menor privilegio
- Use encryption em repouso e em transito
- Configure network security adequadamente
- Habilite audit logging

**Operacao diaria:**
- Configure alertas proativos
- Implemente runbooks para operacoes comuns
- Realize testes de disaster recovery
- Monitore custos regularmente

**Otimizacao continua:**
- Right-size recursos baseado em uso real
- Use auto-scaling quando aplicavel
- Considere preemptible instances para workloads tolerantes
- Revise configuracoes periodicamente



**Avisos importantes:**
- [MUTABLE] Precos variam por regiao e configuracao
- Sempre valide em ambiente de teste antes de producao
- Mantenha backups atualizados
- Documente todas as alteracoes

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Billing/Concepts/costoverview.htm
You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
No ambiente staging da CloudNative Inc, qual e a melhor forma de otimizar consumo por workload para microservices-refactor quando o objetivo e troubleshoot e ha restricao de com budget limitado?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id staging \
  --display-name "high-cpu-staging" \
  --metric-compartment-id staging \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Billing/Concepts/costoverview.htm
You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
Estamos em cenario de incidente e eu atuo como security lead. Como balancear custo, risco e performance para disaster-recovery-setup em eu-frankfurt-1 sem perder de vista com auditoria em 30 dias?

**Integration Pattern — Cost Optimization (FinServe Digital - disaster-recovery-setup)**

**Servicos OCI que integram com cost-optimization:**
1. **OCI Vault**: Encryption keys e secrets management
2. **OCI Monitoring**: Metricas e alertas
3. **OCI Logging**: Logs estruturados
4. **OCI IAM**: Politicas de acesso
5. **OCI Notifications**: Alertas e eventos

**Padrao comum:**
```
Recurso OCI → Events → Service Connector Hub → Destination
                                    ↓
                        ┌───────────┼───────────┐
                        ↓           ↓           ↓
                  Functions   Notifications  Streaming
```

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'sandbox'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'sandbox'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group sandbox-functions to manage objects in compartment sandbox
Allow dynamic-group sandbox-functions to use secret-family in compartment sandbox
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Billing/Concepts/costoverview.htm

Vamos configurar cost-optimization para seu ambiente:

**Fase 1: Setup Inicial**
- Inicie no Console OCI acessando o servico desejado
- Selecione o compartment `shared-services` em `uk-london-1`
- Confirme que voce tem permissoes IAM adequadas

**Fase 2: Provisionamento**
- No Console, inicie a criacao do recurso
- Nomeie como `cost-optimization-shared-services-004`
- Defina os parametros necessarios para seu caso

**Fase 3: Ajustes Finais**
- Ajuste configuracoes de rede e seguranca conforme necessidade
- Aplique tags para organizacao (project=RetailMax Online-compliance-audit)
- Configure monitoring e alertas conforme necessidade

**Fase 4: Verificacao**
- Teste acesso e conectividade
- Analise logs e metricas
- Registre a configuracao

**Referencia:** https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Billing/Concepts/costoverview.htm

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
Estamos em cenario de brownfield e eu atuo como platform engineer. Como priorizar acoes de FinOps para database-consolidation em ca-toronto-1 sem perder de vista integração híbrida?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id data-platform \
  --display-name "high-cpu-data-platform" \
  --metric-compartment-id data-platform \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id staging \
  --display-name "high-cpu-staging" \
  --metric-compartment-id staging \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
Sou dba e preciso criar cost dashboard para SmartCity IoT no projeto backup-strategy, em staging/us-phoenix-1, considerando com budget limitado. Qual abordagem voce recomenda?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id staging \
  --display-name "high-cpu-staging" \
  --metric-compartment-id staging \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
Como reduzir custos OCI no OCI para LogiTrack Logistics no contexto de security-hardening, com cenario incidente e requisito de integração híbrida?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id data-platform \
  --display-name "high-cpu-data-platform" \
  --metric-compartment-id data-platform \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
No ambiente staging da CloudNative Inc, qual e a melhor forma de otimizar consumo por workload para microservices-refactor quando o objetivo e troubleshoot e ha restricao de com budget limitado?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id staging \
  --display-name "high-cpu-staging" \
  --metric-compartment-id staging \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

```
                                    ↓
```

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**

```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'sandbox'}

```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group sandbox-functions to manage objects in compartment sandbox
Allow dynamic-group sandbox-functions to use secret-family in compartment sandbox
```

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
Estamos em cenario de brownfield e eu atuo como platform engineer. Como priorizar acoes de FinOps para database-consolidation em ca-toronto-1 sem perder de vista integração híbrida?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id data-platform \
  --display-name "high-cpu-data-platform" \
  --metric-compartment-id data-platform \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id staging \
  --display-name "high-cpu-staging" \
  --metric-compartment-id staging \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
Como reduzir custos OCI no OCI para SmartCity IoT no contexto de backup-strategy, com cenario expansão e requisito de com budget limitado?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id staging \
  --display-name "high-cpu-staging" \
  --metric-compartment-id staging \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
No ambiente data-platform da LogiTrack Logistics, qual e a melhor forma de otimizar consumo por workload para security-hardening quando o objetivo e standardize e ha restricao de integração híbrida?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id data-platform \
  --display-name "high-cpu-data-platform" \
  --metric-compartment-id data-platform \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
Estamos em cenario de produção estável e eu atuo como sre. Como priorizar acoes de FinOps para microservices-refactor em us-phoenix-1 sem perder de vista com budget limitado?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id staging \
  --display-name "high-cpu-staging" \
  --metric-compartment-id staging \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
Qual desenho voce sugere para analisar cost trends no projeto database-consolidation da FoodChain Trace, considerando standardize, integração híbrida e operacao em ca-toronto-1?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id data-platform \
  --display-name "high-cpu-data-platform" \
  --metric-compartment-id data-platform \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
Sou cloud architect e preciso otimizar compute costs para SmartCity IoT no projeto backup-strategy, em staging/us-phoenix-1, considerando com budget limitado. Qual abordagem voce recomenda?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id staging \
  --display-name "high-cpu-staging" \
  --metric-compartment-id staging \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
No ambiente staging da SmartCity IoT, qual e a melhor forma de otimizar consumo por workload para backup-strategy quando o objetivo e troubleshoot e ha restricao de com budget limitado?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id staging \
  --display-name "high-cpu-staging" \
  --metric-compartment-id staging \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
Estamos em cenario de incidente e eu atuo como security lead. Como priorizar acoes de FinOps para security-hardening em ca-toronto-1 sem perder de vista integração híbrida?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id data-platform \
  --display-name "high-cpu-data-platform" \
  --metric-compartment-id data-platform \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in cloud cost optimization. Provide technical guidance on rightsizing, storage tiering, autoscaling efficiency, waste reduction, and forecasting.
Qual desenho voce sugere para analisar cost trends no projeto microservices-refactor da CloudNative Inc, considerando troubleshoot, com budget limitado e operacao em us-phoenix-1?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id staging \
  --display-name "high-cpu-staging" \
  --metric-compartment-id staging \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'sandbox'}

```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group sandbox-functions to manage objects in compartment sandbox

```
